Hrmh, I see. Here I thought they'd blown away entire continents. That's all the questions I have.
What about you, Your Majesties?
Did we hear you right? The distress signal was sent more than 130 years ago?
The META Elizabeth finally chimed in after remaining silent until this point.
To be exact, 136 years, 3 months, and 13 days.
...It took us less than a day after receiving the signal to rush here.
Less... than one day?
Did our reply reach you?
Yes. Half a year ago now...
We sent it an hour before our departure.
How could this have happened...?
Are you aware that we helped this world's Elizabeth build a time-flow synchronisation machine?
Her Majesty's precious machine... I don't know anything about its functions, but I may know its current location.
Can you take us there?
Yes. It's in the fifth combat sector, being guarded by my fleet of mass-produced ships.
Was that the first area to be taken by the enemy?
Absolutely not!
Ah... I remember now. It started malfunctioning immediately after the extradimensionals appeared...
I recall her doing everything she could to repair it.
You mean... It malfunctioned before she was sunk, and not destroyed after?
We must see it. Now. Take us.
We're going with you.
Okay! I'll take you there!

A World Without Elizabeth.say: Queen Elizabeth META and company took the Queen's Light in order to reach their destination as soon as possible.
Lion's group, save for Cleopatra who stayed behind to stand guard on the Mesektet in the sky, also boarded the train.
Glorious META was brought into the control cabin to lead the way, while all remaining members went into the fourth car to rest.
Whew. What a pleasant train. It feels like being back home again.
Using mobile space-expansion technology for a train lounge... How exorbitantly luxurious.
It really is. Even on the Mesektet, we only use it in the area where all the critical equipment is concentrated.
Hah! You haven't seen anything yet. That technology is but a small part of what makes up these Mirror Rails.
That's incredible. Incidentally, there's something I've been meaning to ask you ever since we met.
It may be rude, but I simply need to know.
Go ahead. I've got questions of my own.
By all means. First question: Are you really Queen Elizabeth?
What kind of question is that? Your version of me may be gone, but surely you still have pictures of her?
The only times you appear in the history books are as a name.
Yup. I haven't seen an illustration or anything.
I've been to many test sites, and this is the first I've heard of something like this. What do you mean?
Does your world draw a difference between the concepts of "warship" and "human"?
Look around you. Could you not ask such basic questions?
Well, you see... In our world, Queen Elizabeth sank in the fight against the Sirens not as a human, but as a warship.
Those Sirens are truly despicable...
Indeed.
So then, the Sirens were enemies in your world, and you fought them?
Was that the case in yours?
More or less.
And yet, Glorious said that the Sirens were their allies in this world. How is that possible?
It depends on the test site. To elaborate would require a long-winded explanation of what the Sirens truly are, so I won't.
That's fine. We already defeated them anyway.
You defeated them. Interesting.
So? What became of your world?
If I'm not there to lead you, I presume King George V took my place.
No... King George V, the warship, was also sunk by the Sirens.
Those bastards. Excuse me, I should say, my condolences.
Then what happened? With neither me nor George around, I presume our Parliament is in charge, then?
Correct. Our Realm is run by the two houses of Parliament. That being said, the two aren't as powerful as you think.
Alright. Let me make a guess.
You introduced yourself as "the governor of the Nile." Do you rule in a separatist manner?
Hahahah. "Separatist." How libelous. I would simply say that my authority is amply respected.
Oh, I'm sure. Respected. What's your next question?
Hmm. That would be...
Ah, actually, wait. I'm curious about something.
What became of Warspite and Valiant? What about Hood, Illustrious, and Vanguard? Or Duke of York and Prince of Wales?
Also, who's the current head of the Royal Maids? Who commands the Royal Knights?
That's a lot that you're curious about.
From my perspective, this is like looking at the world after I died. You must understand.
Wales is the Realm Prime Minister, Hood is the Minister of the House of Lords, and Vanguard is the commander of the Palace Guards.
I'm surprised Vanguard's position stayed the same.
Hearing about myself from a parallel world is a rather... strange experience.
Hahah. The kind of experience you can only get by leaving your home.

Duke of York is the Governor-General of the Maple Monarchy, Warspite is the Governor-General of the Pacific Ocean, and Illustrious is the Governor-General of the Mediterranean Sea – as well as the direct supervisor of Trafalgar.
Governor-generals, eh... I have considered that before. Seems you were the ones to implement it.
So Trafalgar works under Illustrious, does she? Is she on this mission with you, then?
No. However, this mission was supported by both the Imperial Prime Minister and both houses of Parliament.

On the day this expedition was voted on, I happened to be absent, and it was decided that I would be the one to lead it. It was also decided that the Imperial Centre and signing governors would provide me with a certain amount of support.
It sounds to me like they conspired against you.
Hah. Had I been present, I personally would've volunteered for the task. I will go down in history for this grand expedition.
I see. You'll earn quite a lot of fame if it's successful.
Heh. Precisely. By the way, Newcastle is the head of the Royal Maids, while Royal Oak is the commander of the Royal Knights.
Finally, there's Valiant. She is the Governor-General of Terra Australis and the Solomon Islands. I've heard she's pursuing a third governor-generalship.
Goodness gracious... That's so like her.
What about Bel?
Sank by the Sirens as a warship.
Thus, this is my first time seeing you, Belfast.
I had an inkling of that when you said that Newcastle holds my would-be position.
Since Her Majesty is no longer with you, it makes sense that I am neither – for the sake of the Sirens' script.
May I ask a question now?
Of course.
Alright – were you both together with Elizabeth META?
Are there two Elizabeths in your world?
No. She comes from another test site.
I can probably guess why you asked that question. Sadly, the answer is: It's not possible.
How unfortunate. What about the other METAs?
You have enough of them for an entire fleet.
They each have their own story, likely similar to that of Glorious. If you want to know more, you'll have to ask them yourself.
I see. If the opportunity arises, I will.
Ding, ding, ding! Baaang! Boom, boom, boom!
Attention! We're almost there! Get ready to disembark!
Boom, boom, boom! Ding, ding, ding! Baaang! Boom, boom, boom!
What an... eccentric announcer.
Did she change it to Miss D's bloody voice pack?!
Ugh... Forget it. Just think of it as some weird sound clips.
Come on. Let's get off.

Buds of the New Moon.say: The group disembarked the luxurious train, stepping back into the desolate world.
The ground beneath them wasn't much different from what they'd seen so far, but up in the ashen sky, something new caught their eyes.
It was a collection of pupae surrounded by winding, sepal-like metallic structures. Each one silently hovered in the air, as if suspended by invisible strings.
What... are those?
They don't appear to be extradimensionals. Glorious, are they some weapon of yours?
Not quite. They are what we call the "Buds of the Full Moon."
They showed up around the time of the meteor shower long ago and appear exclusively on the Royal Islands.
They appear and disappear with no apparent regularity, and when they return, there's always more of them.
Initially, there were just a handful. Now, I think there are around... 300?
We scanned them with the train. Those sepal-like casings are likely a safety mechanism, and we have no idea what's inside them.
Have you ever tried to destroy them?
The Sirens tried and failed to destroy even a single one.
However, the attempt did cause a devastating explosion that not only obliterated the Sirens' safety bunker, but also flattened the entire anchorage of Scapa Flow.
As you might expect, nobody dared touch them after that.
Despite their intimidating appearances, they simply float there, doing nothing.
Putting them aside, over there is where the device is located – this is the Fifth Wireless Communications Laboratory.
Glorious META gestured toward a large building not far from there.
Wireless Communications Laboratory? That's a good cover name.
Her Majesty perhaps thought it was amusing.
Now that I think about it, one could say that I managed to make contact with you thanks to a kind of wireless communication.
The Laboratory is in a rather poor state of maintenance, so it would take a lot of work to search through it.
For that reason, I mobilised some heavy obstacle-clearing machines from a force nearby when we departed.
But, for better or worse, the train got here so fast that the machines haven't had time to do their work. Please wait for them.
When we departed... Wait, where is the Mesektet?!
Lion was sure she had ordered the Mesektet to follow the train after departure, and yet, it was nowhere to be seen now that she had arrived.
It fell behind because it's just too slow.
Don't panic. I see it on the onboard radar and it's fine. It'll arrive sooner or later.
Hah. So we got here quickly only to have to wait for the machines to finish.
We can do more than just wait. We shall perform a scan from the outside.
Elizabeth META activated the train's scanner. Seeing the results, she made a gloomy expression.
You look ill. Lady Elizabeth, have you discovered something unfortunate?
We found the device. It's in the Laboratory's underground section.
The section is in good structural condition. Why, even some of the security systems are still operating.
However... its two most important components are missing: The tracking indicator, and the inaccuracy synchroniser.
Missing? Did someone damage the device?
Yes, and whoever did so knew exactly what they were doing.
Those components were sealed inside the device when it was completed. Only its makers would be able to take them out without breaking the exterior.
And yet, the exterior is perfectly intact, and the internals are gone.
It's little wonder this world's Elizabeth couldn't fix it. She couldn't have anticipated such a confounding issue.
So the world's hope was destroyed by human hands...
Who could have done this?! Can you locate the perpetrator?!
...We shall try.
In that instant, time stopped.
Begin examination.
All the surroundings expanded in different directions, forming countless overlapping images.
Elizabeth META hunched forward and began looking for the exact moment the crucial change happened.
Hrmh.
Alright, there it is.
Let us see who committed this horrific deed.
Her examination continued, but looking around, she saw only nothingness.
...What?

sequence: Gate of Avalon - Silver Mirror Lake

Inspecting the Whale.say: Four hours after the departure of the Queen's Light, Helena META and Memphis META arrived at the Gate of Avalon on schedule.
There, they boarded a car of the train left behind by Queen Elizabeth META and dove into the Silver Mirror Lake alongside Miss D.
The train door slowly opens, amidst Miss D's yelling and the announcements using her voice data.
After Helena and Memphis exit first to confirm it's safe, I walk out alongside Miss D and take my first steps into the mysterious whale.
Assistant! Assistant, look, look! This is my whale!
It's big, huh?! WAY bigger than Elizabeth's dumb train!
As soon as we've alighted, Miss D turns to me proudly. She acts as if she knows everything about the whale, but I know that isn't true.
Go play with Death's Shadow, okay? I need to look around with the METAs.
Okay! Let's go, stalker! My assistant has important work to do!
After calming her down, I start by surveying my surroundings.
My first impression of the whale's interior is that it's a space of raw chaos.
This space, designated by Elizabeth as a safe place to search, is approximately 300 meters in length and 200 meters in width.
The highest point is nearly 100 meters high, but some spots only have two meters of vertical space.
Most striking of all, though, is that all of the structures and machines in here hang down from the ceiling.
So? Any thoughts?
My first reaction... is that it certainly wasn't made for human use.
Clearly not.
Is there anything else, though? Any sense that you've seen it before, or any memories it might bring back?
Not at all. It's like I'm seeing a tourist attraction for the first time.
Hmm.
Commander, I pulled up a part of the floor and found a control panel. Can you try authenticating?
You... pulled up the floor?
Ahem... I did it safely after a sound structural analysis. I'll put it back when we're done, I promise!
I follow her to the exposed control panel.
Nothing changes on the panel.
...Nothing happened?
My Antiochus system credentials have always come in handy when I needed them, but this time, they've failed me.
Sorry, Helena, but I don't think this lead will get us anywhere. Have you found anything?
I've been trying to hack into the system here since we arrived.
I have found... one thing. When Devil seized this place, she added a bunch of things to the security layer.
I'll have to remove them one by one.
Elizabeth told me that Devil's been trying to take total control of the whale.
Does that mean that she left pitfalls to stop others from catching up while she's hacking it?
Devilish, isn't it?
Honestly, yeah.
Commander! I've uncovered another control panel. Can you try again?
Shh. Keep this up, and Miss D's going to notice. Belay digging around for now and wait for Helena to work her magic.
Yes, Commander.
RRRUMBLE...
Hmm? Do you all hear that?
It's like an earthquake...
GAAAH! This is bad! The whale's escaping again!
Escaping? Are you saying that the whale is turning on?
Yeah, yeah! Who turned my whale on?! Out with it!
Something is terribly wrong. Commander, we must retreat at once.
Oooh, yeah. We gotta get outta here, or else the whale's gonna take us with it!
RRRUMBLE...
Its struggles quickens, as if it's desperately trying to set sail for new worlds.
As it rumbles, Helena, Memphis, and even the Queen's Light's cars begin to blur as the whale tries to expel them.
She's got us. Memphis, stay by me.
Protect the Commander!
Trust me, I was already planning to... Go, Compiler.
A blue current rushes forth, and Compiler appears, equipped with her rigging.
Orders received: Protect the Commander at all costs.
Don't leave your post until you hear from me.
Helena!
Commander, don't leave Compiler's side. I'm going to find you ASAP.
The shaking intensifies further until the METAs disappear. I can barely feel the presence of Compiler and Miss D...
And then, everything goes silent.
The world turns a pure white.

Danger Intensifies.say: When Elizabeth META began her examination, the world underwent great change.
The Buds of the Full Moon, which typically only flickered in or out of existence, began to glow one after another.
Extradimensionals that lay dormant across the world suddenly turned violent. Glorious received countless alerts from more than twenty combat sectors, all at once.
Everyone, the situation is rapidly deteriorating. Extradimensional hordes have awakened – the likelihood of a Beastly Tide has dramatically increased.
It's too dangerous to remain here. We must return to the Rose Tower and plan countermeasures.
Rapidly deteriorating, you say? Is it related to the pupae suddenly glowing?
I can't be certain about anything yet. Your Majesty, we must ready the train for departure.
...Miss Elizabeth?
Elizabeth META did not respond to her address.
This is bad... Your Majesty, she can't hear us!
It was then that everyone noticed something off about her.
Her eyes were closed tight, and she didn't react to anyone's calls. It was as if she'd fallen into a deep sleep while still standing.
Even I've never seen something like this. Sheffy, what's wrong with her?
Her Majesty is in a very distant place. Her consciousness may be temporarily absent.
There must be a big secret behind the destruction of the synchronization device.
Lion, Glorious. The other Elizabeth is busy examining the destruction of the device, so she won't be able to act for the time being.
Let's split up into two groups. Sheffield, lead the METAs to protect this place until she completes her examination.
Everyone else will board the Mesektet and return to the Rose Tower. I trust you'll allow us to board, dear governor?
I'd be offended if you didn't!
You'll leave her alone here? Perhaps she could be transported to the Mesektet and brought back to the tower?
You mustn't remove her. Do not worry – we will keep her safe.
Oh... Then I'll dispatch some Pawns to protect her.
Sounds like a plan. Chop-chop, Cleopatra. Land the Mesektet.
Yes, Miss Governor. Descending now!
Meanwhile, somewhere in the Tower...
Position indication jammed again. It must be Magician.
Tch... I should have kept watch here and had Memphis take Vestal with her instead.
Stay calm, Helena.
If this is her doing, then the Commander's life shouldn't be endangered.
Even still, that doesn't mean that there's no danger whatsoever.
That insane woman's directly picking a fight with me.
No... She activated that trap the instant I left. The timing is too convenient.
Devil may not need the whale anymore thanks to her, but that doesn't mean she'll readily give it away to anyone who wants it.
But Magician's direct interference would change things. I see... That's why Devil kept the inside of the whale under such scrutiny once it was in Elizabeth's hands.
Is she working with Devil, or is she simply using her? I don't know yet, but...
Ugh... I've been blind. Even if Devil no longer needs it, she isn't the only one who would have a use for it.
Magician isn't just after the whale. She's after the Commander, too...
But why?
This is awful. I can't sit idly by any longer.
I just hope that this expedition can supplement the data missing from the Tower's database...
...I'll need to pass more information on to the Ashes.

Exploring the Unknown.say: Once the light fades, I find myself in a wasteland I've never seen before.
The dark-blue whale has turned a yellowish black and shrunk to about two meters in size. It floats a few centimeters above the ground, making it look like an amusement park ride for children.
Heeey! Assistant, look! I can ride it!
I can even fly around on it!
My stalker's gone, too! This trip turned out pretty rad!
Woohoo! Whale flying, whale flying!
Miss D gleefully rides the whale. She apparently sees this terrifying situation as a fun little trip.
Meanwhile, Compiler...
You already built a command center?!
Your compiling speed is jaw-dropping.
Not especially. Confirming information on our environs.
According to the topographical map, we are currently in the Vale of Glamorgan in southern Wales.
Analysis of the land composition yields traces of fierce combat in the past. However, there have been no major battles here in the past three years.
I've also detected a signal left by me – by Compiler.
However, there is no mainframe or similar vessel nearby.
Hypothesis: This is a test site, but the experimentation division has been destroyed.
You learned all of that just now? Impressive.
Not especially. Continuing situation analysis. Recommendation: Go to the nearby building to rest.
No, thank you. I don't get to see you work often, so I'd like to observe.
Do as you please.
Even throughout our conversation, she doesn't stop her analysis.
A flock of microdrones flies out of her rigging. Some go up into the sky, some dig underground, and yet others fly toward the ocean.
What are you doing now?
I am only able to use local resources at this moment. Without my mainframe, my capacity is limited.
Thus, I am searching for repairable and dormant facilities in order to bolster my resources.
If possible, I prefer to build a new mainframe as soon as possible.
...There.
I've found an integrated coastal military module nearby that is worthy of repair. Think of it as a modular base that has not yet been assembled.
...Good. The defense module and mass-production module are both intact.
We can construct a mass-produced escort fleet to defend ourselves.
Not especially.
I was, so yes.
As for a means of travel...
Whale!
Ride my whale! Get on!
Miss D's pleas fall on deaf ears, as Compiler transforms part of her rigging into a sort of hovering armored vehicle.
Whale, or truck?
It's no whale, but your thing's pretty cool, too!
Wise decision. Let us go.
Woohoo! Let's go, whale rider!

Transcendental Insight.say: Elizabeth META wandered through the empty void.
Did we fall into a trap, or did the real perpetrator leave traces when destroying the device?
Narrowing her eyes, she squinted at the nothingness.
Then, she crashed into the earth like a meteorite.
After that, she and the extradimensionals went on a rampage.
They crushed the resistance of everyone in their path – the warships, the shipgirls, and the Sirens.
The world was enveloped in deathly silence, and the space reverted into a void.
...This isn't it.
Must focus harder... Must find the moment the inaccuracy synchroniser disappeared...
One more time, she sharpened her focus and fixated on the nothingness.
In response, the nothingness began to assume a new appearance.
It became the interior of a building with advanced technology. Lights shone brightly and the air was abuzz with voices.
There. We've found it.
Elizabeth walked through the crowds and the walls, heading for the room where the synchronizer was stored.
Then, she saw something – or somebody – standing in front of the device.
It was you who broke it?
What in the world are you?!
Blast it... We need insight!
A ruined wasteland. The extradimensionals formed a horde, and the Buds of the Full Moon were squirming.
The monsters were approaching frighteningly fast, while Elizabeth META was asleep at the device. A team of META shipgirls was the only thing holding the front line.
RUVD FVBY WSHJL (KNOW YOUR PLACE)
A voice from a higher dimension broke Elizabeth's concentration, making the world fall apart for an instant.
...The Star Beast?
Heh. What do you have to do with what we just saw?
NV IHJR (GO BACK)
...Who do you think you are, giving us orders?
Continuing with the insight.
The ruined wasteland again. The Buds of the Full Moon unfolded their sepals.
Cities and the hinterlands, earth, mountains, and rivers – the surfaces of the sepals reflected many landscapes.
(Is... each bud its own artificial Singularity?)
(Did they activate hundreds of Singularities in one world all at the same time?)
(Wait... What is that golden shine? Camelot? And a META Vampire is there?)
(Is that the other Elizabeth's world?)
(And that world, with the radiant lighthouse... Is that where Lion is from?)
(What is this? Do these connect to the worlds of all those who've come here?)
(No, that cannot be true!)
AOL SPNOA VM H MPYLMSF (THE LIGHT OF A FIREFLY)
JHUUVA JVTWHYL AV AOL YHKPHUJL VM AOL ZAHYZ (CANNOT COMPARE TO THE RADIANCE OF THE STARS)
NV IHJR (GO BACK)
The pressure from the higher dimension pushed on her again.
(...The situation demands it. We must see the rest.)
(We must take a risk.)
LCLU H DLHR MPYLMSF (EVEN A WEAK FIREFLY)
JHU WBYZBL AOL SPNOA (CAN PURSUE THE LIGHT)
PUZPNOA (INSIGHT)
Desolation and ruination. Countless wrecks were piled up beneath the Buds of the Full Moon.
The horde of extradimensionals rushed incessantly toward the pupae, only to be torn to ribbons by the entrances of the Singularities.
(They can't pass through the Singularities?)
There was no end to the horde, and their corpses kept piling. This shell of a world was turning into a graveyard for beasts.
P HT AOL ZAHY ILHZA (I AM THE STAR BEAST)
ZOBA BW DPAO FVB (SHUT UP WITH YOU)
PUZPNOA (INSIGHT)
At last, the wasteland had morphed into a world of corpses.
Even the sea was turned white with their remains.
Time passed, and eventually, something changed.
A new adversary was born amidst the wreckages.
It rose from the heap of extradimensional corpses and charged at the Buds of the New Moon.
This time, it passed through in one piece.
One by one, the many scenes reflected in the buds were reduced to silent, dead barrens.
(What is that? Some sort of new mimic entity?)
(A mimic entity... was born from the wreckages of extradimensionals?!)
(Is this the future that awaits this world?)
(Was its fate sealed the moment that the synchroniser was destroyed?!)
(X, it's... No... Could it be?!)
PUZPNOA (INSIGHT)
The world vanished in an instant into the void.
Elizabeth could neither think nor feel. Her very presence began to fade, each moment of time stretching on for an eternity.
JBYZLZ... (CURSES...)
YLABYU AV AOL XBLLU'Z SPNOA (RETURN TO THE QUEEN'S LIGHT)
Elizabeth suddenly collapsed during her examination.
Your Majesty! What happened?! You look dreadful!
Forgive me, I should not have used that word!
Get... Get us out of here...
Take the train... to the Rose Tower...

Compiler the Reliable.say: We arrived at the "repairable and dormant facility" that Compiler spoke of.
On the surface, it looked like a coastal factory that was destroyed years ago.
Compiler commanded her swarm of drones to pick apart the wreckages, then, after leveling the area, new sections began appearing one by one, coming together to form an expanding territory.
First she established a factory, then an airport, a regular port, a radar site, strong defenses with massive cannons, and many more buildings I can't name.
The factory got to work and started churning out small robots.
They joined the swarm taking apart the wreckages, built pipes extending deep underground, and erected embankments and formed rivers.
Finally, she built a tall control tower at the base's center which rose high above.
(The pipes are probably for replenishing resources. The tower, then, should be used to control the Mirror Sea.)
(I'm guessing she's going to use the Mirror Sea as a protective shield for her base.)
While my guess wasn't necessarily wrong, what happened next showed that I'd underestimated Compiler.
After the first control tower was complete, Compiler built a second one.
She encased the Mirror Sea inside another Mirror Sea, then also added a mist consisting of a microlayer medley in between the two. Once that was done, she finally stopped and nodded faintly.
Ah. Commander, I've fortified and camouflaged our temporary base.

Bearing in mind practical access to resource supply and computing resources, I launched five Type II mass-production lines and two Type II Enforcer series production lines. This ensures basic combat capabilities.
For now, we're safe.
Thanks for the compliment.
A weird question. Buying time, obviously.
Most of my resources are currently going toward rebuilding my mainframe.
Hang on, you've even started rebuilding that?!
Its core will be operational in about 72 hours. It can be restarted at 10% of its original capacity.
I have recovered some files about this test site from my surviving mainframe database. Filtering down to the parts that should be interesting to you.
Appreciate it. If there's one thing I need now, it's intel.
So, did you find anything?
I did. The serial number of this test site is SQ-4577486.
The destruction of this site's Compiler was due to an attack by a horde of extradimensionals.
Wait, a horde of extradimensionals?!
I surmise that they already possess the strength to fully take over the world.
Are there any other survivors?
Yes. I've detected signs of a lost mass-produced patrol fleet unaffiliated with the Sirens.
Must be a resistance force, like the one Fritz Rumey led.
Can you pinpoint where their trails converge?
I'm not yet able to perform a worldwide scan. Within my current sensor range, however, they converge upon the port of LD.
...What's this? Commander, something has changed.
The extradimensionals are gathering into large groups. It's likely they're preparing a large-scale attack.
Can you protect against it?
As long as we do not attack first, they are physically unable to detect us.
When will our connection with Helena be back online?
Impossible to estimate.
Then doesn't that put LD in serious danger?
Most likely. Detecting several swarms approaching it. Fighting has already broken out at the outer periphery.
Then we should go and help them.
My orders are to protect you at all costs. This takes precedence.
I still want you to help.
If you insist on sending me to help them, wait until after the 72 hours have elapsed.
Even with only 10% of my original capacity, I'll be able to deploy more powerful weapons and create a path straight from here to LD.
That's too long. The survivors might not hold out for that long, and even if they do, they'll suffer heavy losses in the process.
We have to go to LD and rebuild the line of defense.
The transport process is very risky. I do not recommend it.
What's that? You're not gonna listen to the assistant? Who do you think's in charge here, huh?!
Assistant, if the albino fabricator here won't take you there, my whale and I will do it!
I'll take you wherever you wanna go!
Albino fabricator...
Yeah, that's right!
If you're going to do it no matter what, fine.
At least wait two hours, though. I need that long to prepare the transport.
Alright. Thank you.
No need. I'm only doing what I must.

A Different Royal Navy.say: Lion's group arrived back at the Rose Tower with the Mesektet. Glorious ran straight for the war room and began commanding the counterattack across all combat sectors.
It reminded Elizabeth and Lion alike that the Royal Navy of this world was completely different from the one they knew.
Factories hidden in the mountains, river after river dug out in the plains...
By Jove, there are even purposefully flooded sections to speed up the mobilisation of Pawns and mass-produced ships.
They've torn the country to shreds.
Even so, we keep fighting.
Our glory would be tarnished if we let them run amok in our own homeland.
Miss Elizabeth, I've spotted a large group of enemies in the direction of the Fifth Wireless Communications Laboratory.
Are you still unable to reach your META counterpart? Shall I send reinforcements her way?
I still can't reach her.
Fear not, however. Her escorts are the finest there are. It may seem like they're being pushed back, but they're simply being cautious out of a lack of information on the enemy.
Send your forces to the sectors that need them the most.
If we can help in any way, just say it. It's why we came here in the first place.
I concur. Don't walk on eggshells for our sake.
Whilst the enemy is numerous, it is readily apparent that they're uncoordinated.
If they're going to attack the Rose Tower, we may as well meet them outside.
Understood. In all honesty, something odd is currently transpiring in the Wales sector, and I don't know what to do about it.
The Royal Islands are presently divided into 12 combat sectors. Most of my forces are concentrated in areas one through six – the ones surrounding the Rose Tower.
The Wales sector is number nine, and I am frankly at a loss as to handle the situation there.
Say no more. We'll deal with it.
Would you happen to know anything about what's happening there?
Shortly after the extradimensionals appeared, I detected signs that a Siren fleet had come back online in the area.
Wait, a Siren fleet? Didn't they completely vanish from this world over 100 years ago?
Correct. However, I am sure that it is them – I caught footage of swarm drones repairing one of their former factories.
My latest intel shows that they've formed a fleet and are currently cutting through the horde of extradimensionals.
They've entered the flooded region through the Bristol Channel, and I believe they're heading toward the Rose Tower.
If they're the Sirens from your world, doesn't that make them allies?
Most likely, yes, since despite them having sighted my monitoring stations en route, they did not destroy them.
Here. These are pictures that my hidden cameras took.
Glorious brought up numerous photographs on the war room's screen.
Hm... Yeah, those are Sirens for sure.
Judging by their make, they're mass-produced ships and Enforcers of the Type II variety.
What the–?! Is that a baby whale among them? It's adorable!
...Did you say, "A baby whale"?
Your Majesty... doesn't it greatly resemble the stuffed whale that Miss D has?
Also, while the picture is rather blurry, isn't that outline of Miss D herself, riding the whale?
What... in the...
...This is bad news.
Lion, start the Mesektet at once! We must go to support them immediately!
Hm? First time I've seen you this stressed.
Is that little whale an acquaintance of yours, Lady Elizabeth?
I'll explain on the way. There is no time to waste.
Alright, alright.
Um, your destination is in an active combat zone. Are you sure you want to take the Mesektet?
I'll set it down in the water before we get there. If we're going to fight, we'll do it on our home turf.
A wise decision.
Cleopatra, ready the ship! We're setting off!
Understood!

Invasion Plan.say: The Mesektet's thorn-like, winding metal structure retreated, a curtain of orange light lifted, and the Mesektet finally landed on the Rose Tower.
I did not see a welcome party of Pawns like Elizabeth described, no doubt due to the dire situation on the front. Glorious META offered her apologies for this.
Naturally, Elizabeth and I both assured her that it was fine that she didn't have the leeway for a grand reception.
On the way here, Elizabeth shared with me as much intel as she could. Compared to what little Compiler gathered, she broadly understood what calamity was taking place in this world.
Some of it I'd seen in Rumey's world before – extradimensionals attacked, and the Sirens were destroyed.
In this world, however, the Star Beast hadn't shown itself.
Additionally, the extradimensionals in this world are much stronger than those of Rumey's world, as we learned while carrying out Compiler's plan.
I speculate that the reason the Star Beast didn't show up was that the extradimensionals already present were enough to wipe out the Sirens. It never needed to appear.
While examining the battlefield map in the war room, it hit me how the Royal Navy defined its sectors in an unusual way.
The map didn't use the modern-day regions of the Royal Islands, but rather the ones from the Heptarchy period of its history.
I asked Glorious about it, and she explained with an awkward smile: "This way, it makes the Rose Tower the heart of all the combat sectors."
Indeed it does. The Rose Tower is the core of this world's resistance force, so it only makes sense to divide the areas of operation around it.
She has been fighting like this for a hundred years. She fought until she was the last one standing.
If that isn't emblematic of her name, I don't know what is.
Sometime later, the Queen's Light also landed by the Rose Tower, carrying Elizabeth META.
She had some rather concerning news to share.
The Buds of the Full Moon were going to bloom one day, which would open a tunnel of Singularities connecting to countless worlds.
X's mimic beasts are born from the wreckages of extradimensionals, and they will eventually push through the Singularities and bring destruction to all those worlds.
Two especially notable worlds are linked to those Singularities: Test site beta, and Lion's homeworld.
However, the one who stole the inaccuracy synchronizer as well as the Star Beast have yet to show themselves.
Since Elizabeth exhausted herself by overusing her power, she'll have to stay on the train and rest for a while.
It's all up to us now. We need to do something.
The horde of extradimensionals has the Buds of the Full Moon. It's almost like they're waiting for the sepals to open.
It's proving rather probable that what Miss Elizabeth META said is true.
I never would've thought there existed something even more terrifying than the extradimensionals...
Worse yet, these foul creatures will invade many more worlds through the buds, wreaking destruction on your homes as well.
I'm truly sorry. I did not anticipate that I would embroil you in this disaster...
Do not be sorry. We are sworn allies.
Nobody has said the buds cannot be destroyed. We need merely destroy them all before it's too late.
The collateral damage that stopped you from destroying them before no longer matters. In fact, it may help to reduce the enemy's numbers.
How many Buds are there across the Royal Islands? Can we even be sure that this is the only place where they exist?
Back when I still had contact with other countries, I verified that they had appeared nowhere else.
As for how many there are... There is no exact figure. I don't have many monitoring stations across sectors 8 through 12.
However, based on current data, I estimate that there are somewhere over 400 of them in existence.
Over 400... It'd be impossible to destroy them all.
The solution is right under your nose.
Why must we consider destroying ALL of them?
Because each and every one of them connects to a whole world.
Regardless, our power is limited.
It's simply a fact that we cannot save everyone.
...You're right. We can only do so much.
Still, I will stop at nothing to destroy the bud that leads to my world. Whatever it takes.
Elizabeth META, can you locate those two buds?
Of course. We remember them vividly...
We sorted the coordinates after we got back.
*cough cough*... They've been processed. Sending them to you now...
BEEP BEEP!
Coordinates received, Miss Elizabeth.
Let's see... One is in the southwest of Wales, by the waters of the Vale of Glamorgan. The other is on the Isle of Wight in the Wessex sector.
It's close to the location where you first arrived.
(The Vale of Glamorgan – that's where Compiler erected all those structures.)
(Then I don't need to worry THAT much about the bud there.)
The sooner we deal with the risk, the better. Let's split up.
I concur. We should each dispose of the bud connected to our own world, yes?
No, because it'll make one of our teams significantly weaker. You'll struggle, Lion.
Yes. You are correct.
Ahaha... Then you'll have to share some of your forces with us.
Take my META counterpart's subordinates with you. I, my own subordinates, and Compiler will accompany my servant here.
No... Your group shall go with Lion, whilst we will come with the Commander.
We are the only one able to pilot the Queen's Light... Going separately from us expands your selection of tactics.
You're right. I overlooked that.
Alright – servant, you'll go with my META counterpart. I will bring my followers along to support Lion.
Good. Now we have two balanced teams.
Ideally, we should bolster the Rose Tower's defenses as well. For all we know, the Star Beast might show up out of nowhere.
Hunter, you'll stay behind.
Roger. Glorious, tell me if I can help with anything.
Much obliged.
And so, we split into two groups and boarded the Queen's Light and the Mesektet, respectively, and departed from the Rose Tower.
Elizabeth, Belfast, Vanguard, Renown META, and Repulse META will provide support for Lion.
Meanwhile, Elizabeth META will be accompanied by her subordinates along with Miss D, Compiler, and me.
(This will be my first time commanding both Sirens and METAs at the same time.)
(That sure sounds like the kind of lineup the United Council fought X with.)
(Alright. Time to take these mimic entities head on!)

In the Name of the Eternal Sun.say: On account of its proximity to the Rose Tower, the Wessex sector has always been firmly under Glorious' control.
Furthermore, since almost all the extradimensionals are moving toward the Buds of the New Moon, the airspace between here and the Isle of Wight was comparatively clear.
This allowed Lion to pilot the Mesektet to the Isle in record time. Once we disembarked onto the sea, the shipgirls assumed battle formations and engaged the enemy in direct combat.
Whew... Once we're below the bud, we can set down the high-yield explosives that Elizabeth META supplied us with.
I brought extra just to be safe, and asked Glorious to provide aerial support. One might call it double insurance.
I think her explosives should more than suffice. Knowing her, she probably already gave you more than needed.
Better to be safe than sorry when so much is at stake. I also brought my own bombs for the purpose of the expedition.
I thought I'd use them to blow up a mountain to make a base or somesuch. Instead, they'll be used to bring down a giant.
We spent unimaginable amounts of resources to construct just one Singularity, and here are over 400 ones just "growing in the wild."
And now, I must destroy one of them with my own hands, lest everything I hold dear vanish in a heartbeat.
Heh... Just yesterday, I was sitting by the bank of the Nile, looking forward to a new era.
Today, I'm caught in a battle to the death with an incomprehensible foe, facing a crisis that involves the survival of my world.
How do I put it? It feels like being in a bizarre dream.
The situation makes me want to tender my resignation. I have no idea how I will write a report on all this when I return...
Thinking about writing reports at this time? You must utterly adore your job.
My mind is a mess currently. The only way I can calm myself down is by thinking about work.
You seem like you're used to high-stakes situations such as this, Your Majesty. Care to share some nuggets of wisdom?
Me? Do I really look like I'm taking this well?
Hmm. Perhaps I am. I've been through so many life-or-death battles that I stopped counting.
Everyone's life is different, so while I can't give you any advice on dealing with stress, I can share my mindset with you.
My homeworld was a Siren test site. It was also a prison we could not leave.
Sure, a prison where you'd be fed and clothed, but there was danger everywhere if you took one step outside.
Even if a prison is comfortable, should you be content with living and dying by the whims of its warden?
In our case, we eliminated the warden and gained some freedom for ourselves.
That you did. Now, are you going to let your world be destroyed by an unknown danger, like what happened to those reflected in the buds?
It's dangerous outside the cage. We all knew that. But now, we have freedom.
And with freedom comes the right to choose.
You can learn about your enemies, you can grow stronger, you can explore the unknown – you can keep chasing after hope.
One day, you'll have gotten through the true danger, and then, a glorious future won't seem like a pipe dream anymore. Do you know what I mean?
A noble perspective. Thank you.
I think I can picture the outline of that report now.
Glad I could help.
As for the distress signal we both received...
What about it?
The signal we received contained a message: "In the name of the Eternal Sun."
What is this "Eternal Sun"? Is it some sort of code word used in your alliance?
Something like that. According to my other self, it's a plan to recruit allies from beyond worlds of our own – the Eternal Sun Project.
Then I would like to formally request to join. I want to be part of this project.
Do you mean as a representative of your country, or in your capacity as the Governor of the Nile?
I'm not sure yet... For now, the latter.
That can be arranged. As to-be allies, I naturally have no plans of meddling with the affairs of your world.
You're permitted to join the Eternal Sun Project. Alternatively, we can form a temporary alliance.
However, I ultimately do hope to establish a formal alliance with the entirety of your Royal Navy.
You see what I'm driving at, don't you?
Of course. I pray that we can form a cooperative arrangement.
Good. Since we're true allies now, I want to ask something that's been weighing on me.
You said you "defeated" the Sirens – does that mean you destroyed all of their mainframes?
Like the one under the ice sheets in Antarctica. Did you deal with it?
Meanwhile...
Unlike the well-defended Wessex that Lion's team was passing through, Wales and Cornwall had almost nothing in the way of allied forces stationed there.
Not only would fighting there be extremely dangerous, but it was also impossible for the Queen's Light to cut right through it to our destination.
The silver lining was that we had more than enough firepower.
The combined onslaught of the Sirens and the METAs was ruthless. The extradimensionals were forced to fall back.
Soon after, we'd reached our destination below the bud.
(This was a lot easier than when I fought alongside the Iron Blood's Resistance.)
We got here fast this time! Good job, albino!
And you, Elizabeth! Good job on you, too!
Praising us now, are you? Stop being cheeky, lest we suspend your snacks.
Nooo! I'm sorry! Uh... How are your wounds?
Just fine. There is yet to be any sign of the bud opening, but anything could happen. Keep your guard up and let's go.
Your Majesty, Compiler has begun placing the high-yield explosives!
Are you sure bombs will really do the trick?
It's hard to tell how durable the inside is on account of how well-isolating the sepal is.
However, based on the destructive power of her weaponry in previous missions, we think it'll be more than enough.
We also have a backup plan of bombarding the bud from a distance with the train's weapons. You need not worry.
So much is on the line here, though. I can't help but worry until it's been thoroughly destroyed.
I stare at the Bud of the Full Moon floating in the air.
All of a sudden, the sepal opens and the bud begins to bloom. Its inside reveals a continuously changing space.
The very next moment, Miss D's whale just as suddenly grows rapidly in size – from 5 meters, to 10, to 50...
The whale leaps into the air, Miss D still riding it, and it charges straight into the center of the bud.

Bridge to the Beyond.say: The world is vast, dark, without bounds.
I feel as if I weigh nothing as Miss D and I are sucked into it.
(What in the world...)
(Where's Miss D?!)
I frantically search my surroundings and find her floating alongside Death's Shadow nearby.
(Wait... I'm floating, too?)
It feels as if we're soaring through the void alongside the whale.
(My mind isn't being torn away from me or dissolving into the void. Is the whale protecting me?)
(Whatever the case, where are we going right now?)
I try calling out to Miss D, but she doesn't react, as if she's deep asleep.
After an unknown stretch of time, stars finally begin to dot the empty blackness.
Buds bloom, and various fragments pass us by.
Among them, I see Fritz Rumey.
She's leading a Resistance fleet to take back lost homelands after the repair of the purification device and defeat of the Star Beast.
The enemy's formation is breaking. Don't let up! Drive them back once and for all!
(Does this bud connect to her world?)
Next, I see Raffaello.
After the restoration of the Sardegnia League, she holds a personal exhibition in Florence. Her paintings display scenes from her dreams.
Most conspicuous among them is a portrait of a girl wearing a black crown.
Wanna know exclusive, never-before-seen things about Her Holiness Marco Polo? Come see my exhibition!
(I see this one goes to Raffaello's world.)
Images weave into one another like an endlessly morphing dream.
No... This isn't a dream.
They're all reality. They're events happening right now in the worlds that these buds are connected to.
If the bud blooms and the mimic entities get into each world... then it's all over.
Over 400 buds. Over 400 worlds.
I could split my forces into two groups. One to destroy the bud leading to Rumey's world, and the other to destroy Raffaello's...
But what comes next?
Do we just wait and watch while every other world is destroyed?
Every world has its own history, its own people, and its own future.
I can't be complicit in this.
But...

sequence: How many sacrifices are worth it?

Bridge to the Beyond.say: I'm too powerless.
Even with all of my resources, power, and knowledge...
It's nothing in the face of real crisis.
I can't give up.
This is all wrong. I have to do something about it!
"This is all wrong."
"I have to do something about it."
What?!
BEEP BEEP!
Engaging Death's Bridge.
BEEP BEEP!
Switching to real-time communication.
BEEP BEEP!
Attempting communication.
What... are you doing?
There is no response. Miss D and Death's Shadow continue their mechanical, mirrored soliloquy.
BEEP BEEP!
BEEP BEEP!
BEEP BEEP!
Hello? Death? Why have you contacted me through Death's Bridge?
What's going on? Do you need me to come there?
...You're silent. Are you in a situation where you can't speak freely?
Don't worry. Remain calm. I'll be there right away.
Call ended. Closing Death's Bridge.
That voice was so familiar...
Huh? Why did you push that button?
What did you screw up this time, Hierophant?
Now I remember.
It's her...
The Chariot VII!

Greater Plans.say: Suddenly, I find that I'm beneath the Bud of the Full Moon again.
Miss D is still on her tiny whale, and Compiler's still setting up high-yield explosives. It's as if everything in that dark space never happened.
(Everything's quiet. Nobody noticed a thing, but I'm certain that everything that just happened was real.)
Did you just experience something... uncanny?
(Huh?!)
Elizabeth? Did you see it, too?
No... Our level of insight has fallen for the time being due to our injuries. We only noticed thanks to a warning issued by the Queen's Light.
Now, what did you see?
Ah, wait a moment. Leave the experience aside and only state your conclusion for the time being.
In short, what must we do?
The two buds we were originally planning to destroy won't be enough.
We need to destroy all of them. Don't leave a single one intact.
...Even were we in tip-top shape, this would be outright impossible.
Our combined efforts can destroy more, yes, but we still must pick and choose.
Don't worry. Reinforcements are on their way.
...Reinforcements?
Gaaaah! Assistant, Elizabeth! The bud... It's moving!
What?!
As indicated, the sepals on the Bud of the Full Moon slowly open. The horde in the distance begins to writhe in unison with them.
Oh, no... We don't have much time until it blooms.
Let's destroy this one as planned right away. Then, we return to the Rose Tower... We'll have Lion's fleet meet up with us there.
Things are changing by the second. We need to make a new plan!
Later...
The first stage of the operation went smoothly. Our high-yield explosives had successfully destroyed the Bud of the Full Moon.
Everyone successfully retreated to the tower before it exploded, as well.
However, many other buds around the Royal Islands unfolded their sepals and bloomed, creating Singularities that connected to other worlds.
The extradimensional horde washed upon the buds like a tidal wave before being ripped to shreds by them. It was exactly like what Elizabeth META had seen.
During this meeting, I tell all of our allies about the need to destroy every single bud.
I add that the Arbiter named Chariot is on her way to this world with an army of Antiochus.
Hmm... Destroying every bud in order to protect every world, eh?
You're ambitious. I like that.
Lion, you can't be seriously considering...
I haven't said yes yet.
Commander, your goals are lofty, but you haven't convinced us all that they're feasible yet.
Let me ask two questions before I'm satisfied.
Go on.
You've told us about the relationship between the Sirens and Antiochus, as well as the experimentation division.
But first, when is Chariot coming? Will she be here before the mimic entities appear?
Second, can we even rely on her? Or is she going to kill us along with them?
I don't know exactly when she'll be here, but we were using an emergency communication channel.
Given the Arbiters' usual quick action, she should get here on time.
Regarding your second question... The X and the Antiochus always fight until one side is eradicated.
Even if she did try to kill us too, she'd only do it once the X threat was all gone. We should have plenty of time to escape.
I'm hearing a whole lot of "should."
Lives are on the line here. If you cannot be sure yourself, I am not convinced either.
...Though, I do like your courage. As big of a gamble as it might be...
If we win, we can save hundreds of worlds and build a relationship with an Arbiter.
If we lose... Hah. I'm happy to accept the fruits of victory, but I'm not interested in the burden of defeat.
If things look bleak, my people and I take our leave. Don't blame us for that.
I won't. We're ready to retreat if necessary, too.
I want to save as many worlds as I can, but I also want to avoid unnecessary sacrifice. That includes myself and my friends.
Good. Then let's discuss details.
Our only ray of hope is Chariot leading an army of Antiochus in and annihilating all the mimic entities.
But we don't know when they'll arrive, and once enough corpses pile up, the mimic entities are guaranteed to come.
What can we actually DO here?
We can spread out the extradimensional corpses as much as possible.
If we draw their attention and disperse them across a wider area, rather than piling up their corpses near the Buds of the Full Moon, we may delay their arrival.
Even if the mimic entities do appear, we can buy time if they appear in an area of our choosing rather than near the buds.
In that case, what if we draw them toward the Rose Tower itself?
It is a symbol of the Royal Navy and this very world. As long as it stands, we will live on forever.
Any hostiles who encroach upon us, land or water, are those who hope for our downfall.
And if that be their desire, then they must first destroy the Rose Tower.
Hmm? A symbol of the Royal Navy...?
No way... Was this tower a conceptual weapon all this time?!
Indeed. If I change it to interception mode, the horde is sure to target it.
When they do, we can divide them into combat sectors and conquer them one-by-one.
Not a bad plan... Your thoughts, servant?
We have the home-field advantage by the tower. A defensive battle is the best place to take advantage of Compiler's strengths, too.
Let's go with this strategy.
Then I will change the tower's mode. Give me three hours, if you'd please.
This will be our final moment of rest before a battle without end. Relax as much as you can manage, everyone.

Admiral Mikhail.say: As the Rose Tower entered charging mode, the walls of nearby buildings were gradually encased in light.
Having temporarily left the front, Glorious used the remaining time to host a tea party, hoping to socialize with the other METAs.
Lion and Elizabeth went together to discuss the details of their alliance. They didn't want to leave ambiguities in the event of an emergency retreat.
As for me, I went to Repulse META and Renown META to talk about the things that happened in the past.
...I should add that my intention with this wasn't to stop them from going to the tea party.
It's just that Repulse calmly murmured that she "can sympathize with the girl, but at least she had tea to drink for the past 100 years." She didn't seem too keen on going to the tea party.
I'm glad no one else heard that sad line.
So you wish to know what happened in world alpha...
As you may or may not know, everyone who left alpha, including us, has more or less forgotten everything about that place. Or rather, we have false memories.
Furthermore, we are completely unaware of this issue until we realise it on our own.
What this means is that, frankly, even I am not sure how much of my memories are true.
That being said, I do vividly remember the thing you want to know about.
"Fight until the last light has faded" – that was indeed the order that the Council gave us at the time.
You may also already know that there are no survivors from the Northern Parliament among the Ashes.
The reason for that is very simple. They all sank in battle.
All of them...
And you called ME driven by obsession! You should have a good look in the mirror!
Project Dawn is the biggest obsession of them all!
You think it's your ray of hope? You couldn't be more wrong. It's an express ticket to your downfall.
Just being close to it brings misfortune.
Do you know what happened to the people involved in this project? I can tell you.
Don't answer. I'll tell you either way.
The project planner went out with a fleet to test a new experimental device. Then... Poof! None of them were ever seen again!
Then the project was suspended and became an accessory to a separate project.
And when that project started, our entire fleet, as well as our last admiral, were all sacrificed! Not a single survivor!
"Fight until the last light has faded."
That's what they all said. They all fought to their deaths and let someone else have their chance to live!
This is what Project Dawn is! This is the obsession that's been passed down!
Was their sinking related to Project Dawn? Why did the Northern Parliament give up on retreating?
It was because someone had to protect the Auroral City and maintain the weather conditions required for a retreat.
The Parliament had always been the one pushing for Project Dawn. It was also the Parliament that volunteered to perform this final undertaking.
After the "fight until the last light has faded" order was issued, the Parliament's final naval commander – Admiral Mikhail – took his fleet and set sail.
That was the last thing I witnessed with my own eyes, and it was the end of the Northern Parliament in world alpha.
Admiral... Mikhail?
(Hang on a moment...)
If we adopt this plan, there may be a chance of success.
However, we must ensure that the jamming device remains operational as long as possible. In the meantime, we’ll have to hold the line while evacuations are carried out.
In other words...
We already know this, Dr. Aoste. We’ve taken it upon ourselves to build upon this framework.
"The meeting is now adjourned. Unfortunately, our proposed amendment did not pass the United Council's vote."
"As a result, we will continue with our original plan instead."
"It is time to make a decision, General Commander."
(That's it! It was him!)
(This is so strange. It's like some of the fog over my memory lifted as soon as I heard his name.)
(I can't quite remember how he looked, though.)
(Wait... Something else is coming back to me...)
Activating system.
Connecting to command system.
So, Admiral Mikhail – are you pleased with the results of this collaboration?
Hmm. I had a look at all the testing data. I must say I'm impressed.
The collaboration with the Dragon Empery yielded Moon, the one with Iron Blood yielded Fortress, and the one with the Royal Navy yielded Enlightenment. Now our motherland has created Scythe.
This gives the War Protocol series a fourth unit. Work can finally begin on creating the Council's new task force.
I eagerly look forward to seeing their performance in battle.
(Scythe, Fortress, and Moon – they were in the recording Hierophant showed me.)
(But who – or what – is Enlightenment?)

Tea Party with Glorious.say: The Rose Tower's glow became brighter and brighter as the charging continued.
The sky was illuminated faintly, providing a novel backdrop to this pre-battle tea time.
The cannon fire at the outer perimeter's getting louder...
Should we really be sipping tea here while Compiler's erecting defences as fast as she can?
The enemy will not come after us until the Rose Tower's mode change is complete.
Do not be alarmed, Grenville. There is plenty of time.
Precisely. Enjoy your tea while this momentary lull lasts.
Alright...
Heehee. Excessive formality spoils the tea, so why don't we have a little chat?
Sure. Propose a topic.
While you ladies already know my past, I don't know much about yours.
I've never seen this many METAs in one place, either. Would you mind if I inquired about you?
Hmm. Not a half-bad topic for a tea party.
Grenville, you start.
Eh? M-me?!
We know why you're nervous. It's because you've experienced something like this before, no?
Did your world go through something similar to ours, then?
...Many things were different then, but yeah. I did have some quiet time before my big battle.
*sigh*... My home was a test site where we and the Sirens just kept killing each other.
Well, to be fair, they were the first ones to propose peace and shared their technology with us. Then, one day, they suddenly snapped and turned hostile to us.
For all the good times we had back then, we suffered doubly as hard afterward.
Our world wasn't reset... but the hell we went through was even worse.
My friends and I survived thanks to Her Majesty's leadership. She also brought what few survivors were left to safety.
I decided to stay by her side after that and pledged my sword to her.
We speculate that the experimentation division in that world wanted to create a civilisation with a certain degree of scientific ability, tear it down, and cultivate a new one from its ashes.
That's why they didn't utterly destroy it. It's also why Grenville, among others, could be saved.
All this time, I believed the Sirens were unwavering allies... What an extreme story.
There are all manner of experimentation divisions. In the end, they are but machines loyally following the orders given to them.
In some worlds, they are allies. In others, they are invaders one must fight to the death against.
What about Compiler, who's aiding us as we speak? Is she like that?
Ah, her. She's a bit of an exception. Which is to say, she is an ally beyond any doubt.
That's a relief... Erebus, could you share your history with me?
My past isn't much to talk about.
I was wounded in a brutal battle. In my despair, I chose METAmorphosis. Later, Her Majesty came and made me stable.
I've fought under her ever since then.
As for my world, it fared better. The Sirens were defeated before they completed their reset.
Hunter, on the other hand – you were even luckier, weren't you?
A sudden mention...
Yes. My world was a bit unique.
Having walked this path with Her Majesty for as long as I have, it's become apparent how absurdly lucky we were.
Much like this place, we initially coexisted with the Sirens and got on well.
That is, until X's influence began to spread, and mimic entities popped up across the globe.
In any other case, we would've either fought the entities and barely come out on top, or we'd have been annihilated.
However, almost as soon as the fighting began, an Arbiter appeared.
She chose to fight the entities with us, and completely expelled all of them from our world before they could propagate.
The Arbiter then made a decision. All the experiments on our world would end, the experimentation branch would be dissolved, and our very test site would be excluded from the Antiochus' database.
To do this required a worldwide cognitive alteration, erasing every last trace of Antiochus and X.
Then there was I. I had already seen the truth and could not choose ignorance.
So the Arbiter brought me with her. I can remember naught after that point.
Everything that happened after that and which Arbiter it was that saved me – all of it, gone.
What I do remember is wandering the wasteland by myself, followed by meeting Her Majesty and Miss D.
Ever since that day, I've fought by her side.
That is quite the bizarre experience... It certainly seems like the Arbiters are more unknowable than the experimentation divisions are.
They ARE self-alleged machines given complete personalities. It's little surprise they cannot be predicted.
What about you, Sheffield? I heard you, Repulse, and Renown were acquainted. Are you from the same world?
While I do not share a world of origin with them, I have known them for quite some time.
Broadly speaking, we met very shortly after they left their home.
Afterward, the three of us fought for a long time under the same organisation until we got separated during a major operation.
Following that, I met Her Majesty, and decided that I would work with her.
I apologise for the sparse details. To truly explain my past requires too much additional context to fit in a chat at the table.
It's alright. What you've already told me is more than sufficient.
Ah... There really is so, so much beyond this world.
I'd love to hear the second group's stories someday.
It's not as though they can't hear what we're saying at this tea party.
Pardon? What do you mean?
We know, Glorious. We know this tower's secret as well as why you locked yourself away here, alone, for all this time.
I... see.
Glorious pauses for a moment, then takes a silent sip of her tea.
I am the final glory of the Royal Navy... No matter what happens, I must not fall.
At that moment, the Rose Tower pierced the sky with its light. The clouds that covered the Royal Islands for a hundred years cleared up for the first time.
It was though the earth, the hills, the rivers, everything that remained in this land had some sort of connection to the Rose Tower.
The Rose Tower existed on the Royal Islands, and the Royal Islands also existed inside the Rose Tower.
As for Glorious, she turned into specks of light and disappeared into thin air as soon as the tower's light shone.
Huh?! Glorious is gone!
*sigh*... We knew this was coming.
Don't worry about her. She just retreated inside the tower to fulfill the duty imposed on her.
...We'd better prepare ourselves, too.
Come. The battle is starting.

Preparing for War.say: When the tower begins to shine for the first time, I've already taken my place in the command center built by Compiler.
The monitor shows an impressive breadth of information on our allied forces.
More than 200 production lines... 172 Type II mass-produced fleets?!
Thanks to the help of Glorious.
She has not fully utilized the facilities under her control, but she has kept them maintained.
With the reconnaissance data collected over the past hundred years, I've discovered the resource reserves and backup plans created by this world's Compiler and Omitter.
Within six hours, I can start at least 100 production lines and reform at least 70 Type II mass-produced mixed fleets.
In a sparse few hours, she would have us ready to go to war.
It all looks like ruins to me, but you see resources in it all...
The Antiochus are incredibly efficient at utilizing resources.
By the way, can you only produce Type II models? Can you create flying fleets?
Unfortunately, I do not have the Council's permission to do so.
Okay. What's the most powerful model available to you?
The standard Type V model.
I thought IV was the last one, but they have Type V now?
Higher model numbers require more complex production processes, increasing both resource and time costs.
Type III will suffice for experiments in test sites. Type IV is only for reserve use, and Type V is only for extreme situations caused by factors beyond the experiment.
Do not be misled by the current data. The only reason we can mobilize so many forces in such little time is that we're consuming existing reserves.
Without support from a mainframe, sustained production is unlikely, and battlefield micromanagement will be limited.
That is why I've chosen Type II mass-produced ships as simple cannon fodder, prioritizing our goal of stalling for time.
I see. Prioritizing raw numbers... Yeah, I like that plan.
Though our ultimate goal is buying time, that doesn't mean we can afford to focus all of our forces around the Rose Tower right away.

We have multiple combat sectors available to us, and the enemy has no unified control structure. That means that intercepting them in the outside sectors is the ideal strategy, using space to buy more time.
As the person most familiar with the battlefields here, Glorious will take charge of everything from the Rose Tower. Elizabeth, Belfast, and Vanguard will assist in defending the first combat sector.
Sectors two, three, and six will be defended by Compiler's Siren fleet, assisted by Renown and Repulse – those most experienced with fighting alongside Sirens.
Miss D and I will be working with Compiler through this process.
I put Lion's team in charge of the fourth sector. They'll use the Mesektet's mobility to secure strategic advantages.
The fifth sector is vast enough for the Queen's Light to exploit, so I assigned Elizabeth META's team to it.
At the same time, Compiler will operate Siren facilities in surrounding combat sectors and other sea regions to disrupt the enemy on a wide scale.
Combat commencement signal received from Glorious.
Per your request, I have transferred authority over one-third of the Siren fleet in the second combat sector to you.
Permissions in this command center have been opened to you as well.
We will remain here and command the battlefield until it is time to retreat to the Rose Tower. Do you object?
Not at all.
Good. Then let us begin.

By a Hair.say: KABOOOM!
The mimic entities were indeed far more powerful than the extradimensionals.
In just three hours of fighting, our defensive line had been pierced in several places. Glorious chose to abandon all outside fronts in favor of focusing our firepower.
Apart from the ever-mobile Queen's Light and Compiler's ability to summon Siren mass-produced fleets from odd positions, the Rose Tower's barrier was all we had left.
A fierce battle raged outside the barrier. We could hear enormous explosions from even inside the tower.
It was then that Elizabeth summoned me to a chamber of the tower.
Servant. The situation is only worsening.
Our last line of defence is in a precarious state. There's no sign of Chariot coming to save us.
It's time we seriously consider a final retreat.
Should we use the Queen's Light and Camelot's stealth mode, we should be able to evacuate everyone safely.
We can take Lion's party and Glorious alike.
Whatever the case, our decision must be swift. Escape will become much more difficult when the barrier breaks.
As it stands, we can last six more hours... No, maybe even longer.
The METAs aren't made to be on the receiving end of a siege, and Compiler isn't much help under these circumstances.
The longer we fight, the more of a risk we're taking.
We've done everything we can. Relying on the Antiochus will only get us so far.
Besides, they only care for their own needs. They don't care what sacrifices it takes as long as they've killed the X in the end.
Whether it be us, you, or their own selves.
They're simple killing machines that stop at nothing, in the name of "salvation."
*sigh*... I understand that you've made contact with the Antiochus through some channels unknown to me, so maybe you consider your relationship a good one...
But think about it. What if everything you've been through is just another part of their plan?
Further, how can you be certain that they haven't discarded the plan entirely?
Our world and Lion's world are safe. The META version of me destroyed the buds of the two worlds you were particularly concerned over, too.
Of course, it's cruel to simply abandon all of the other 400 worlds. I'm not saying I WANT to.
But we must face reality.
Just surviving and protecting the people we love is already too much for us to handle.
If we die for nothing today, then what will they do?
The world is constantly in danger, be it at the hands of Sirens, of the X, of the very people of that world, or of some other thing we've never faced before.
Things happen in places we can see and in places we can't.
We're no gods, and we're no messiahs.
That may be, but even if we can't change the results, we can still make a difference.
We can't turn a blind eye to tragedy just because it's going to happen either way.
We've decided that the Sirens' methods are wrong, and because of that, we walk a different path. To be indifferent is to be the same as them.
You're right. That's why I continue to search for allies, and that's why you've saved other test sites.
It has to be done one step at a time. We can't expect to fix everything in one fell swoop.
If you must, then you may regret today's losses, curse your own powerlessness, and swear that you'll never let it happen again.
But right here, right now? Impossible means impossible.
As I recall, you said, "We're ready to retreat if necessary."
Yeah. You're right, I'll admit.
But... I'm afraid I just need more time. Our friends are still holding out – it's not time to beat a hasty retreat, not yet.
I had a feeling you'd say that.
Did Elizabeth META put you up to this?
Half and half, really... You get six hours, and not a minute longer.
Six hours. When that time comes, we're leaving no matter what.
That's all I needed. I'm going back to the battlefield.
Also, I hear some among us are less willing to wait. They plan to leave ahead of us.
Not to name any names, but they're the kind of girls who don't fold before my royal authority. You'd best think of how to calm them.
Immediately after Elizabeth's departure, Lion rushes over to me.
(I had a feeling.)
Commander, I've been looking for you!
We've been fighting for long enough. Is this Arbiter of yours coming or not?
Chariot will come, I assure you. I just can't tell you when.
You said that last time. Our fight has gone on for two straight days. Everyone is at their limit.
Those mimic entities – the X – are foes you'll have to learn to fight sooner or later.
I understand that it's difficult, but consider this necessary experience for your future survival.
Agreed. The thing is, we can't do anything with that experience if we die in this hellhole. Who's going to tell the people back home what we've learned here?
(Hmm... Is it just me, or does it seem like she isn't quite demanding to leave early?)
I still have many more great exploits to accomplish. I won't lay down my life or those of my shipgirls for a pointless venture.
Good news. Elizabeth and I have decided that we're retreating in six hours. Sorry I didn't lead with that.
Whether Chariot comes or not, we have other ways to share information on the Sirens and Arbiters.
Even putting Compiler aside, Miss D and her whale are inextricably tied to the Arbiters.
...Six hours, eh?
Very well. Then I'm going back to the Mesektet to keep up the fight. See you then!
(Elizabeth and Lion are set on retreat.)
(So even Arbiters aren't especially speedy when it comes to dealing with problems across worlds.)
(If Chariot isn't here in six hours, then we'll have to abandon this world.)
(The buds leading to Rumey's resistance and the Sardegnia League have already been destroyed.)
(If we can evacuate everyone, including Glorious META, then this mission could be considered a success. But...)
(She's been fighting for this world all on her lonesome for so long now.)
(Will she willingly leave alongside us?)
(I need to discuss this with her.)

Glorious's Choice.say: Glorious hasn't taken one foot out of the war room ever since the Rose Tower switched to interception mode.
However, a passage has opened for carrier aircraft that connects the Rose Tower and the outside. It allows her to dispatch her air wings without issue.
Hello, Commander. Are you here to discuss the retreat?
You knew about it?
Miss Elizabeth and Miss Lion each went to meet you, and now you've come to me. I assumed that's why you're here.
I've heard that the Sirens give serial numbers to their test site. You and your group are from "test site beta," correct?
What kind of world is it?
That's a... pretty vague question.
If you ask me, it's a beautiful world filled with life, hope, and endless possibilities. A word that overcame the fate imposed on it.
Overcame the fate imposed on it... A beautiful world filled with so many lovely things...
That's wonderful. It must be even better with someone as great as your Miss Elizabeth around.
Do you happen to know the serial number of this world?
Test site SQ-4577486. I see.
This, too, once was a beautiful world filled with life, hope, and endless possibility.
Commander. You did not give up on your home – neither will I give up on mine.
You've crossed paths with many a META.
I'm sure some of them have given up on their worlds, while others are looking for a new place to call home.
I respect their decisions and wish them the best.
I, however, cannot and will not give up.
In truth, you don't want to give up on this world either, do you?
If you did, you wouldn't have persuaded the others to keep fighting as long as they can.
Even so, things are what they are. Nothing will change even if we give it our all – that's your reasoning, so you reluctantly agreed on a retreat.
...I'm sorry, but yeah, that's the logical decision I arrived at by considering the circumstances. I just cannot wager all their lives on this.
Logical, you say... Logic is not what has kept this tower standing for a hundred years.
You are wrong, Commander. You've not yet seen the true limit of the Rose Tower.
This does not scratch the surface of our strength.
The noble girl has moved to the center of the hall, sword in hand.
I've seen the sword before. It was in Elizabeth's treasure collection.
Is that... the Sword of Mercy?
I am the final glory of the Royal Navy. I shall now let it shine radiantly.
O souls resting beneath the tower, O prisoners captive within it...
I awaken ye...
For one final, glorious battle...
The originally sealed-off hall begins to expand outward infinitely.
Amid countless rooms, countless fragments of light glitter.
I see the grand ambition that Elizabeth had when the war began.
We'll build a great tower! A tower able to protect all of the Royal Islands! And its name shall be – the Rose Tower!
(...This is the moment the tower was born.)
As the war turns increasingly violent, the land beneath the tower becomes a resting place for heroes.
...We shall keep fighting. Until we achieve flawless victory, or our last drop of blood spills.
Rest in peace, Lady Hood.
(And this is Hood's funeral...)
One funeral after another is held, until eventually all the mourners themselves end up in a grave.
A suffocating loneliness hangs in the land beneath the Rose Tower.
I'm the only one left.
I must not die before I carry out my duty...
I simply... must not die.
I am Glorious, the bearer of the Royal Navy's glory.
As long as I breathe, its glory remains intact.
Yes... I understand now...
Heed me, Rose Tower: I will be your protector.
Together... we will protect the Royal Islands.
(...Glorious is the tower, and the tower is her.)
Lights and shadows begin to gather in the endlessly expanding space.
There are faces in them – some familiar and others unknown, some brimming with life and others devoid of it.
They glow and sway gently, almost like candleflames blowing in the wind yet just barely staying lit.
Each time a light or shadow appears, the light given off by the tower grows even brighter.
Its barrier becomes sturdier yet and it begins emitting light with a destructive power to its surroundings.
The mimic entities, along with their vector zones, are reduced to dust in the light. For a while, the area around the Rose Tower becomes a silent no man's land.
Far away, the sea begins to billow, and a furious Rising Tide advances on the Royal Islands' coastlines.
Long-since sunken wreckages of mass-produced ships of the Royal Navy and its allies rise to the surface.
Those still with functioning propellers charge desperately toward the enemy, while those with working cannons bombard them instead.
Lastly, the ones with only power left martyrize themselves in an explosion.
Even the ones reduced to nothing but wrecks ride the waves toward land, battering any enemies they collide with.
The carcasses of the extradimensionals and even the mimics' vector zones are all destroyed and diluted by the impact of this Rising Tide.
While I can't explain it, I just know that this is a miracle created through the sheer faith of the Royal Navy.
A miracle of the power of thoughts.
